info.title: Creating and analyzing a core dump

abstract.para: In this section you force an application to quit without a proper shutdown. Thus the application creates a core dump that you can use to learn how to investigate core dumps.

topic.para: The following procedure uses Vim to trigger a segmentation fault (segfault) for testing purposes.

step.para: Ensure the debuginfo-pool and debuginfo-update repositories are active for your registration and enabled.
Install the debug information for vim.
To trigger a crash, open a file with Vim, then from another terminal, send a SIGSEGV signal to the process:
step.screen: ps ax | grep vim # kill -s SIGSEGV <PID>
step.para: vim shows error messages, if any.
step.screen: Vim: Caught deadly signal SEGV Vim: Finished. Segmentation fault (core dumped)

step.para: Verify if the crash was captured:
step.screen:
coredumpctl TIME PID UID GID SIG PRESENT EXE Wed 2019-11-12 11:56:47 PST 2345 1000 100 11 * /bin/vim
coredumpctl info PID: 2345 (vim) UID: 0 (root) GID: 0 (root) Signal: 11 (SEGV) Timestamp: Wed 2019-11-12 11:58:05 PST Command Line: vim testfile Executable: /bin/vim Control Group: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/session-1.scope Unit: session-1.scope Slice: user-1000.slice Session: 1 Owner UID: 1000 (tux) Boot ID: b5c251b86ab34674a2222cef102c0c88 Machine ID: b43c44a64696799b985cafd95dc1b698 Hostname: linux-uoch Coredump: /var/lib/systemd/coredump/core.vim.0.b5c251b86ab34674a2222cef102 Message: Process 2345 (vim) of user 0 dumped core.
Stack trace of thread 2345: #0 0x00007f21dd87e2a7 kill (libc.so.6) #1 0x000000000050cb35 may_core_dump (vim) #2 0x00007f21ddbfec70 __restore_rt (libpthread.so.0) #3 0x00007f21dd92ea33 __select (libc.so.6) #4 0x000000000050b4e3 RealWaitForChar (vim) #5 0x000000000050b86b mch_inchar (vim) [...]
step.para: An asterisk * in the PRESENT column indicates the dump file is available on disk.
To view the stack trace and metadata of the latest crash:
You can filter core dumps by PID, COMM (command), or EXE (full path to the executable).
To filter all core dumps for Vim:
To filter single core dump by PID 2345:
To load a specific crash for PID 2345 directly into the debugger:

section.title: Managing Core Dump Storage
section.para: To minimize the storage of sensitive information, you can disable disk storage and log only to the journal by modifying /etc/systemd/coredump.conf. If the field is empty there is no stored core dump, and coredumpctl retrieves crash information from the journal. You can control this behavior in /etc/systemd/coredump.conf with the Storage option:

listitem.para: Storage=none Dumps are logged in the journal but the memory image is not saved to disk.
Storage=journal Cores are stored directly within the systemd journal.

section.para: Core dumps are not preserved after a system restart unless moved. To save a core dump to a permanent file:
For more information, see man systemd-coredump, man coredumpctl, man core, and man coredump.conf for complete command and option listing.
